title: Corporate Crush

jokes.text: Your face... it's like you're happy or something.
jokes.setup: Frank notices something unusual about Liz's appearance
jokes.punchline: He's disturbed by the concept of happiness itself
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Frank - treating happiness like a disease. Economy and character integration are excellent.

jokes.text: 'Cause of that dude you're donking. Floyd.
jokes.setup: Frank trying to understand Liz's happiness
jokes.punchline: Uses crude slang 'donking' in his typical frank way
jokes.explanation: Frank's characteristically blunt sexual reference. Good character work but not particularly original.

jokes.text: I thought it was moving... my bowels.
jokes.setup: Someone says a movie was 'moving' in emotional sense
jokes.punchline: Frank redirects to literal bodily function
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection joke with perfect timing. Structure and economy are excellent, though the concept is familiar.

jokes.text: Reverse, Reverse, Skip, Skip, Draw Four!
jokes.setup: Someone playing what sounds like an intense card game
jokes.punchline: It's just UNO being called out with dramatic intensity
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor in treating a children's card game like high-stakes drama. Good economy but limited character integration.

jokes.text: So, if you ever need someone to come over and videotape you guys doing it or whatever, I can make room in my schedule.
jokes.setup: Frank offering relationship support to Liz
jokes.punchline: His 'support' is offering to film them having sex
jokes.explanation: Perfectly in character for Frank to offer inappropriate 'help' while being genuinely supportive. Excellent cringe comedy.

jokes.text: No judgments.
jokes.setup: Frank just offered to videotape sex
jokes.punchline: He assures there would be 'no judgments'
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up that compounds the absurdity. Frank's sincere delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Look at those eyes... sharp like a panther's.
jokes.setup: Don Geiss commenting on Jack's appearance
jokes.punchline: Compares eyes to a panther's with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: Establishes Don Geiss's eccentric speaking style. The animal comparison is absurd but delivered seriously.

jokes.text: I got a welt on my ass the size of a Red Delicious apple.
jokes.setup: Don Geiss explaining why he can't sit down
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific apple variety comparison for his jellyfish sting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specificity with 'Red Delicious' - the extra detail makes it absurd and memorable. Great character voice.

jokes.text: As my old man always said, 'If you try, you win.' And he was a hell of a garbageman.
jokes.setup: Don Geiss sharing inspirational wisdom from his father
jokes.punchline: His father was just a garbage collector, undermining the gravitas
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that undercuts the inspirational quote. The earnest delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: But I'm taking the Microwave Division away from you.
jokes.setup: Don Geiss expressing faith in Jack
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts it by removing his responsibilities
jokes.explanation: Classic corporate doublespeak misdirection. Perfect structure and timing.

jokes.text: His wife looks just like Walter Matthau, but she's always there for him.
jokes.setup: Don Geiss talking about the importance of marriage
jokes.punchline: Compares someone's wife to Walter Matthau as a positive
jokes.explanation: Walter Matthau is a hilariously specific and unflattering comparison delivered as a compliment.

jokes.text: What's that film where he turned into a dog? Uh, 'Fat Bitch,' sir.
jokes.setup: Don Geiss asking about Tracy's movie
jokes.punchline: The movie has an absurdly crude title delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: The absurd movie title contrasts with the formal business discussion. Good shock value.

jokes.text: I call it 'Jefferson.' A movie version of 'The Jeffersons'? I love it. No, Thomas Jefferson.
jokes.setup: Tracy pitching a movie called 'Jefferson'
jokes.punchline: The obvious assumption is wrong - it's not the TV show
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays on audience expectations. Clean structure.

jokes.text: You want to play Thomas Jefferson? And Sally Hemings and King George. I'm gonna play all the parts.
jokes.setup: Tracy wants to play Thomas Jefferson
jokes.punchline: He escalates to playing literally everyone in the movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy ego escalation. Character integration is excellent - only Tracy would think this way.

jokes.text: What's up, stupid jerks? I'm Thomas Jefferson.
jokes.setup: Tracy demonstrating his Thomas Jefferson portrayal
jokes.punchline: Jefferson greets people as 'stupid jerks'
jokes.explanation: Tracy's modern vernacular as a founding father is perfectly absurd and in character.

jokes.text: I'm gonna get us one of those big clocks, and I'm gonna hang it in there.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his $35 million budget needs
jokes.punchline: His major expense is hanging a big clock somewhere
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and random detail that shows Tracy's misunderstanding of movie production.

jokes.text: Aw, you got a face like a baby's bottom. Poop!
jokes.setup: Complimenting someone's face as smooth as a baby's bottom
jokes.punchline: Immediately adds 'Poop!' to ruin the compliment
jokes.explanation: Classic Lutz - well-meaning but sabotages himself. Good character moment.

jokes.text: It makes me feel unsafe, like when my mom used to make daiquiris and sing Tanya Tucker songs.
jokes.setup: Someone explaining why Liz's good mood makes them uncomfortable
jokes.punchline: Compares it to specific childhood memory of drunk mother
jokes.explanation: Specific enough to be funny, but the reference feels somewhat random.

jokes.text: Jack goes to Sbarro when he's angry, the New York Stock Exchange when he's horny, and Christie's auction house when he's depressed.
jokes.setup: Explaining Jack's emotional coping locations
jokes.punchline: Each location perfectly matches his personality and the emotion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character observation that's both funny and revealing. The Stock Exchange for horniness is inspired.

jokes.text: To be perfectly honest, the first couple of people I did that to were not you, but... here we are.
jokes.setup: Jack sensing someone behind him without turning around
jokes.punchline: Admits he was wrong about who it was the first few times
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack - turns a mistake into a smooth admission. Good deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: I took the money from the sale of those pieces, and I bought a sailboat. And I named it after my ex-wife, and I sank it.
jokes.setup: Jack bought a sailboat with money from selling ex-wife's jewelry
jokes.punchline: He named it after her and then deliberately sank it
jokes.explanation: Dark, petty, and perfectly Jack. The escalation from buying to naming to sinking is brilliant.

jokes.text: It's true... the Bianca Blows is somewhere at the bottom of the Peconic Bay.
jokes.setup: Following up on sinking the boat named after his ex-wife
jokes.punchline: The boat name 'Bianca Blows' is both crude and accurate
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that adds the pun element. The crude double meaning works well.

jokes.text: I wish I were a horse... strong, free... my chestnut haunches glistening in the sun.
jokes.setup: Jack looking at equestrian paintings while depressed
jokes.punchline: Gets oddly specific and sensual about being a horse
jokes.explanation: Hilariously uncomfortable moment showing Jack's mental state. The 'chestnut haunches' detail is inspired.

jokes.text: I'm not a creative type like you with your work sneakers and your left-handedness.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why he can't do creative work
jokes.punchline: Identifies 'work sneakers and left-handedness' as creative traits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack - reduces creativity to superficial observable traits. Brilliantly specific and character-driven.

jokes.text: His name is Floyd. That's unfortunate.
jokes.setup: Liz correcting Jack about her boyfriend's name
jokes.punchline: Jack immediately judges the name as 'unfortunate'
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack snobbery delivered with perfect economy. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Let's say Priscille, 9:00... be sure to wear a tie.
jokes.setup: Jack wants to meet Floyd at dinner
jokes.punchline: He tells Liz to wear a tie to the restaurant
jokes.explanation: Jack's corporate formality extends to telling his female employee to wear a tie

jokes.text: Well, that doesn't even make sense. Everyone knows Fat Bitch died at the end.
jokes.setup: Tracy being asked to do Fat Bitch 2
jokes.punchline: Kenneth earnestly explains the plot continuity problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth - takes the absurd movie seriously and applies logic. Great character moment.

jokes.text: Well, remember when Fat Bitch called all her dog friends together, and they used their high-pitched howling to mess...
jokes.setup: Kenneth giving Tracy advice
jokes.punchline: He's referencing plot points from the ridiculous dog movie
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the Fat Bitch absurdity. Kenneth treating it as serious cinema is in character.

jokes.text: I'm never gonna finish this bikini before Nana's birthday.
jokes.setup: Someone working on a craft project
jokes.punchline: They're knitting a bikini for their grandmother
jokes.explanation: Weird detail that's absurd but not particularly clever. Good economy though.

jokes.text: Liz Lemon, you are my Alexander Hamilton.
jokes.setup: Tracy asking for Liz's help
jokes.punchline: Compares her to Hamilton in a way that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Random historical reference that fits Tracy's ego and pretensions. Good character work.

jokes.text: 'Jack Attack: The Art of Aggression in Business.' Oh, no.
jokes.setup: Floyd mentions reading Jack's book
jokes.punchline: Liz realizes this is bad news
jokes.explanation: The book title perfectly captures Jack's personality, and Liz's reaction sells the joke.

jokes.text: He got here before us. You're not supposed to let that happen. That's chapter two in the book.
jokes.setup: Liz notices Jack arrived at the restaurant first
jokes.punchline: References specific business advice from Jack's book
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Jack's business tactics are so systematic they're in his book

jokes.text: The only other 'Floyd' I ever knew was this Korean barber who used to cut my hair down in the 50th Street subway station. That's my dad. I'm Floyd Jr. I'm just kidding.
jokes.setup: Jack telling a story about another Floyd he knew
jokes.punchline: Floyd pretends it's his dad, then reveals he's kidding
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that shows Floyd can match Jack's conversational style.

jokes.text: The dedicated popcorn setting on your microwave was the imagination breakthrough of 1995.
jokes.setup: Floyd praising Jack's business achievements
jokes.punchline: Calls a popcorn button an 'imagination breakthrough'
jokes.explanation: Perfect corporate brown-nosing language applied to something mundane. Good satire.

jokes.text: And on a personal note, sir, in my mind, the Foo Fighters' song 'Best of You' is about your managerial skills.
jokes.setup: Floyd continuing to praise Jack
jokes.punchline: Makes an absurd connection between a rock song and management
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly random connection that escalates Floyd's suck-up behavior to absurd levels.

jokes.text: Lemon, I want to kiss your boyfriend on the mouth.
jokes.setup: Jack is impressed by Floyd's flattery
jokes.punchline: Expresses his appreciation in an inappropriately physical way
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack line - inappropriate but sincere. The directness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Could you go away for a while? I got to get rid of Freddie's erection.
jokes.setup: Tracy asking someone to leave for a private matter
jokes.punchline: He's talking about a horse's erection, not a human's
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection that takes advantage of the absurd situation with the horse.

jokes.text: Freddie is playing the part of Thomas Jefferson's horse, Caractacus.
jokes.setup: Explaining the horse situation
jokes.punchline: Horse has specific historical character role and fancy name
jokes.explanation: The specificity of the horse having a character name and role in Tracy's absurd historical epic adds to the ridiculousness.

jokes.text: This movie is my destiny. It's the reason why God put me on this Earth.
jokes.setup: Tracy defending his Jefferson movie
jokes.punchline: Claims it's literally his divine purpose
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy ego and grandiosity. Very much in character.

jokes.text: Oh, sorry. I don't shake hands. I have avian bone syndrome. Hollow bones.
jokes.setup: Phoebe declining to shake hands
jokes.punchline: Claims to have bird-like hollow bones
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific medical condition that sounds plausible but absurd. Good character establishment.

jokes.text: They look just like the one crapping in my office.
jokes.setup: Looking at beautiful equestrian paintings
jokes.punchline: Liz connects them to the horse defecating in her workspace
jokes.explanation: Good callback that brings the absurd situation back to reality. Liz's practical frustration is perfect.

jokes.text: Pray, who be this Tracy Jordan thou speakest of? Speakest.
jokes.setup: Tracy is in character as Thomas Jefferson
jokes.punchline: Uses fake old-timey language badly, then corrects grammar
jokes.explanation: Tracy's commitment to bad period dialogue is funny, and the grammar correction adds another layer.

jokes.text: Well, stand guard by his rump and await it in his droppings.
jokes.setup: Horse ate Tracy's wig
jokes.punchline: Tracy/Jefferson suggests waiting for it to come out naturally
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the horse situation. Tracy staying in character makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Aha, I like you, young man. You shall run my university.
jokes.setup: Someone suggests just buying a new wig
jokes.punchline: Jefferson/Tracy immediately promotes him to university president
jokes.explanation: Good absurd promotion that fits both Tracy's impulsiveness and Jefferson founding UVA.

jokes.text: Or perhaps it's eating universal healthcare.
jokes.setup: Someone asks if lion is eating the horse in painting
jokes.punchline: Jack sees political metaphor instead of literal image
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack - sees everything through conservative political lens. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: It's kind of like you two are dating.
jokes.setup: Jack and Floyd making lunch plans without her
jokes.punchline: Liz points out the romantic dynamic between them
jokes.explanation: Good observation that makes the subtext text. Sets up the later conflict.

jokes.text: Unh. It's got pockets. Are you into that? Ooh, what's this? A used Kleenex.
jokes.setup: Romantic moment between Liz and Floyd
jokes.punchline: Liz's sexy outfit reveal includes gross details
jokes.explanation: Good anti-romantic comedy that undercuts the mood with gross reality.

jokes.text: 'Cause this VapoRub isn't gonna get under my nose by itself.
jokes.setup: Floyd declining to go out because of VapoRub routine
jokes.punchline: Treats nasal medication application as urgent priority
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor about unglamorous personal care. Not particularly original.

jokes.text: I always sign it 'Floydster.'
jokes.setup: Jack has been calling Floyd 'Floydster'
jokes.punchline: Floyd has adopted the nickname Jack gave him
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Jack's influence over Floyd. Subtle character work.

jokes.text: The call is coming from inside the house.
jokes.setup: Jack calling them repeatedly
jokes.punchline: References horror movie trope about the threatening call
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference. Gets the job done but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: He seems very taken with Floyd. And you, of course.
jokes.setup: Jonathan comments on Jack's interest
jokes.punchline: The afterthought 'and you, of course' suggests Jack cares more about Floyd than Liz
jokes.explanation: Jonathan's polite but pointed observation about Jack's priorities

jokes.text: Well, Tracy... that was terrible. The answer is 'no.'
jokes.setup: Tracy shows his elaborate Jefferson trailer
jokes.punchline: Don Geiss delivers brutal but honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Tracy's grandiose project. The bluntness is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Well, Tracy... that was terrible. The answer is 'no.'
jokes.setup: Don Geiss watches Tracy's elaborate Jefferson trailer
jokes.punchline: Immediate brutal dismissal after all that effort
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation after the elaborate trailer buildup. Geiss's corporate directness cuts through Tracy's delusions.

jokes.text: I don't give a damn about the masks. I'm on all fours trying to shove the corks back in the bottles.
jokes.setup: Jack telling story about airplane emergency
jokes.punchline: Prioritizes saving expensive champagne over oxygen masks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack story that reveals his warped priorities. Visual of him on all fours is great.

jokes.text: Floyd is me 20 years ago. I'm Don Geiss 30 years ago. 20 years from now, Floyd will be me, I'm gonna be Don Geiss, and Don Geiss will be dead.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining male professional relationships
jokes.punchline: Casually notes Don Geiss will be dead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analysis of corporate mentorship with dark comedy. The matter-of-fact death prediction is perfect.

jokes.text: Look at your life, Jack. It's... It's like this skybox. It's fancy and it's empty and it smells like crab cakes.
jokes.setup: Liz criticizing Jack's lifestyle
jokes.punchline: Compares it to their current fancy but soulless environment
jokes.explanation: Great metaphor that uses the setting effectively. The crab cakes detail makes it specific and funny.

jokes.text: ß I'm gonna get an iPhone ß ß Everybody's gonna be jealous ß
jokes.setup: Tracy dealing with movie rejection
jokes.punchline: Sings about getting an iPhone to cheer himself up
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tracy's shallow coping mechanisms. Dated but works for the time.

jokes.text: I mean, who eats 16 flautas after midnight?
jokes.setup: Jack listing his problems
jokes.punchline: Specific late-night eating confession
jokes.explanation: Good specificity with '16 flautas' but not particularly original emotional eating joke.

jokes.text: Oh, God, I hope we're talking about the same thing.
jokes.setup: Phoebe mentions showing him something delicate in private
jokes.punchline: Jack assumes it's sexual
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo joke. Gets a reaction but relies on cheap sexual humor.

jokes.text: Well, I don't think Phoebe would appreciate being referred to as a Floydster.
jokes.setup: Jack referring to Phoebe as his 'Floydster'
jokes.punchline: Liz points out the absurdity of applying Floyd's nickname to Phoebe
jokes.explanation: Mild callback that makes the obvious point about the nickname's inappropriateness.

jokes.text: Um, Phoebe, I want you to be my wife. Wait. What? Will you marry me? No.
jokes.setup: Jack asking Phoebe a question
jokes.punchline: Jumps straight to marriage proposal and gets immediate rejection
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows Jack's impulsiveness. The immediate 'No' is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Ow. I'm sorry. It's okay. Ow. Careful, my bones.
jokes.setup: Jack trying to be romantic with Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Keeps hurting her because of her fragile bone condition
jokes.explanation: Good callback to her bone condition. Physical comedy that pays off earlier setup.

tmdb_overview: The tables have turned inside 30 Rock: Liz is actually happy in her new relationship with Floyd while Jack finds his job may be in jeopardy after his televised fireworks fiasco prompts top GE man Don Geiss to warn he's got his eye on Jack's performance. Tracy vies for Geiss' attention, hoping to turn his feature film idea for Jefferson into a reality.
insight: Observational comedy sustains 77.8 score despite thin standout moments across 62 jokes.
